name: Suffolk Raisin Roly-Poly (English)
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix the flour, salt, and baking powder together.
2. Add the suet and enough cold water to form a soft dough.
3. Turn out onto a lightly floured surface and roll into an oblong about 1/4 inch thick.
4. Sprinkle the raisins and sugar over the dough.
5. Roll up like a Swiss roll, dampening the ends and pressing firmly together to seal.
6. Sprinkle with a little flour, then wrap lightly in greaseproof paper and roll up in a lightly floured pudding cloth, tying the ends tightly.
7. Place in a saucepan of boiling water and boil for 3 hours, topping up the water as necessary.
8. Serve cut into thick slices, sprinkled with a little granulated sugar and accompanied by custard. Serves 4 to 6.
